title: Getting Started
description: Install Hound and process your first job in minutes

blocks.text: Hound is a type-safe, Deno-native job queue backed by Redis sorted sets. It provides at-least-once delivery, concurrency control, retries, cron scheduling, and a management REST API out of the box.

items: Redis sorted sets for reliable, ordered delivery
Type-safe job definitions with full TypeScript inference
At-least-once delivery with an automatic Reaper
Per-handler concurrency limits and queue priorities
Built-in HTTP gateway with management REST API
InMemoryStorage and Deno KV backends for local dev and testing

blocks.text: Add Hound to your project via JSR:
Or add it directly to your deno.json imports:
Create a Hound instance, register a handler, start the processor, then emit a job:
InMemoryStorage is ideal for local development and tests — no Redis setup required.
Pass any Redis-compatible client to the db option for production use:
Hound is a singleton — Hound.create() returns the same instance on repeated calls. Use Hound.getInstance() to retrieve it elsewhere in your codebase.
